title: Rotting Oranges
problem_statement: You are given an m x n grid where each cell can have one of three values: 0 representing an empty cell, 1 representing a fresh orange, or 2 representing a rotten orange. Every minute, any fresh orange that is 4-directionally adjacent to a rotten orange becomes rotten. Return the minimum number of minutes that must elapse until no cell has a fresh orange. If this is impossible, return -1.

examples.explanation: The orange in the bottom left corner (row 2, column 0) is never rotten, because rotting only happens 4-directionally.
Since there are already no fresh oranges at minute 0, the answer is just 0.

intuition: Use BFS starting from all rotten oranges simultaneously. Each level represents one minute. Count fresh oranges and ensure all become rotten.

approaches.name: Multi-Source BFS
approaches.description: Enqueue all initial rotten oranges. BFS level by level, each level is one minute. Rot adjacent fresh oranges.
approaches.why_not: DFS doesn't model time well; BFS does level-order.
interview_commentary: Multi-source BFS: start with all 2s in queue.
Process by levels: each level increments minutes.

interview_qa.a: BFS models time correctly; DFS would need extra tracking.
Yes, but more complex to track time.
